metadata.title:
Of FAKE


POLICE PROBE SHOWN UP

Accusers of the Police Overlooked
Only Victims of the System"


Are Questioned.

pages.raw_text:
Mayor Pratt's police investigating
committee will conclude Its Iabors
tonight. As anticipated, and an
nounced when the committee was
appointed by the mayor, the report
will largely be a whitewash.


When It is recalled that the
names of tho women examined
were furnished by the police, the
witnesses summoned by the police,
and only such witnesses called as
were favorable to the police, it can
readily be seen that the "investiga-
ton" was farce from the start.


The Rev. James w. Kramer, a
member Of Mayor Pratt's ministe-
rial cabinet, who is on the investi-
eating committee, confesses in an
interview in an evening paper that
the committee will whitewash the
police. The Rev. Kramer comes
back today in a second interview,
in which he corrects the first one
in so far as It relates to the Elliott
cas-, to the extent that his mind is
not made up as to the guilt or the
innocence of the two policemen, but
that it looks bad to him at the
present.


The Rev. Kramer is a minister
of the gospel. Had he desired to
follow the teachings of the lowly
NaZarine and see that justice was
meted out he would have called
on and talked to the old soldier, J..
Elliott Lo
wife, Helen Elliott. They are the
parties that have raised their
hands to heaven and sworn
charges of connivance against
Chief Sullivan. of immortality
against Police Officers Hood and
Glider, and conspiracy against
Judge Mann, yet the Rev. Kramer
and his police committee never
even as much as invited them to
appear before them.


Had Rev. Kramer and his fake
committee desired to learn the
truth they could have called in j
| e. Clark, veteran of the Grand
Army, man whose veracty is un-
| questioned, who made an investi-
cation of the Elliott case, at the re-
quest Of former Police Commits
sioner Tuerke, but no-they did
not want to hear from Captain
Clark, and he was not called.


Mr.. and Mrs. Elliott, the old
couple who swore that their home
was wrecked by the two policemen,
said this morning that no member
of the police Investigating commi-
tee had called to see them or in
vited them to appear before that
body. ]. E. Clark says the same
thing. Yet the two women on the
committee had the time to see the
Elliott girl, the defender of the po-
lice, the weak girl, to save whom
old man Elliott and his good, hon.
est wife gave up some of the best
years of their life.


The police Investigating commit
tee was too busy to see Mrs. T.
Swanson, the woman who swore to
Jail conditions that are an abomin.
alton. Neither did they have the
